title: Speed Art with Stable Diffusion Forge UI & Photoshop
description:
Welcome to a unique speed art experience where I take on the challenge of creating a digital painting featuring a dragon in a fantasy setting, using both AI and Photoshop. This video compresses a 70-minute creative session into just a few minutes, showcasing the entire process from start to finish without any initial planning. Utilizing the capabilities of the Stable Diffusion Forge UI with the Juggernaut XL Version 9 model, I experiment with different prompts to develop a captivating scene of a dragon in a cave, emphasizing beautiful colors and compelling composition.

🐉 In This Video, You'll See:

- The Creative Process Unfold: Watch as I navigate through various prompts to perfect the fantasy theme of a dragon in its cave, making real-time decisions on composition and color.
- AI and Photoshop Synergy: Discover how I merge AI-generated images with Photoshop techniques to refine and detail the artwork, enhancing its overall appeal.
- Detailing Techniques: Observe my approach to Inpai
transcript: Today I recorded myself creating a dragon digital painting using AI and Photoshop.  The whole process took about 70 minutes. I didn't plan anything. I just hit record and showed you  how I tackled this concept. I'll speed up the video so it's condensed into just a few minutes.  I'm using the stable diffusion for GUI with the Juggernaut XL version 9 model.  The idea I had in mind was to create a digital painting of a dragon in a cave, something fantasy  I experimented with different prompts until I found one that inspired me focusing on  nice colors and composition to bring the concept to life. Once I have an image I can send it to  in paint and start adding more details in the things I need. I just had some fun playing around,  trying different things to see what could work. I'm exploring different ideas until I find  something I like. For the in painting part you can add new elements but you can also enhance  the details and specific parts of the illustration like I'm doing here with the fantasy flowers.  Since the in paint is set to only masked I can achieve better details on the part I selected  with the brush. Then I simply drag the new image onto in paint again and can select a new area  that I want to improve.  In Photoshop I'm using liquefy to adjust shapes. This method allows me to make things appear thicker  or thinner, move arms in large heads and perform all kinds of adjustments. I liquefy is faster  but I can also use warp or digital painting for similar effects.  What I'm doing here is instead of using in paint I'm selecting parts from my image with the  selection tool and pasting them onto image to image to enhance them. This way I get better versions  of that portion. Then in Photoshop I drag the same image, making sure it's the same size  and use a soft brush to mask out the edges. After that I blend it with the initial image.  This gives me more control over the final result.  This is how the final 4K image looks and now it's time to animate it a little using software called DP  Animation Maker.  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .  .
